ELPAC Level 1
Lexile 650–1000L
DTR · TALK ABOUT A SCENE

Falling Forever Sideways

STS-MS6-L1-6501000-MSSCI6-RWS-LAT-02 • MS Science Grade 6 • Gravity and Orbital Motion in the Solar System and the Milky Way
Look at the picture. You will answer some questions about it. Press the play button to hear each question. Record your answer for each question. You can listen to your answer and record it again. Click SUBMIT RECORDINGS when you have answered all the questions.
JUST FOR FUN · OPTIONAL
Real flight controllers work in rooms that look a lot like this one. High in the Atacama Desert in Chile, scientists watch the sky from one of the driest places on Earth, where the air is so clear that the whole Milky Way arches overhead at night. They track how gravity keeps planets moving in curved paths around the Sun, and how those same rules hold billions of stars together in our galaxy.
If gravity is what keeps a planet from flying off into space, what do you think holds the whole Milky Way together?
Optional. You do not have to record this one.
1
What is Valentina doing?
Look at the picture. Name what Valentina is doing with her hand.
2
What is Mateo holding?
Look at the picture. Name the object Mateo is holding up.
3
What is the teacher doing?
Look at the picture. Name what the teacher has in both hands.
4
What room is this? How do you know?
Look at the picture. Name this room. Then name one thing you see that tells you.
5
Tell me about the big screen.
Look at the picture. Name two things you see on the big screen.
6
What is Lucía wearing?
Look at the picture. Name the color and the kind of clothing Lucía has on.
Record an answer for each question, then submit.
Content alignment: NGSS MS-ESS1-2 — The role of gravity in the motions within galaxies and the solar system. ELD (Speaking — official ELPAC “Talk About a Scene”): PI.A.1, PII.B.3, PII.B.4, PII.B.5.
